pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce R.A.I.N. Lab (Recursive Architecture of Intelligent Nexus), explaining the dual-architecture system (ZeroClaw + James Library), its purpose for AI research and autonomous agents, and high-level capabilities

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Guide users through initial setup, from installation to first successful run

pages.title: Installation
pages.purpose: Explain installation process for Windows (INSTALL_RAIN.ps1, shortcuts) and manual setup (Python venv, dependencies, Rust build)

pages.title: First Run Setup
pages.purpose: Detail the guided onboarding process, including preflight checks, environment validation, and Godot client installation prompts

pages.title: Configuration
pages.purpose: Explain the centralized configuration system, environment variables (LM_STUDIO_*, RAIN_*), Config dataclass, and priority hierarchy

pages.title: Core Architecture
pages.purpose: Explain the system's fundamental design: ZeroClaw (Rust runtime) for performance and James Library (Python) for research intelligence

pages.title: System Overview
pages.purpose: Provide architectural overview showing how ZeroClaw and James Library interact, shared infrastructure, and data flow patterns

pages.title: ZeroClaw (Rust Runtime)
pages.purpose: Document the Rust-based runtime components: agent orchestrator, channel management, model providers, gateway, and memory systems

pages.title: James Library (Python)
pages.purpose: Document the Python research system: RainLabOrchestrator, RLM (Recursive Lab Meeting), research corpus, and agent personalities

pages.title: Service Supervision
pages.purpose: Explain openclaw_service.py's role as a watchdog process: heartbeat monitoring, crash detection, automatic restarts, and task.json commands

pages.title: Launcher System
pages.purpose: Document the unified launcher (rain_lab.py) that routes to different operational modes and manages sidecar processes

pages.title: Unified Launcher
pages.purpose: Explain rain_lab.py's architecture: mode resolution, sidecar management (Godot bridge, client), and process supervision loops

pages.title: Operational Modes
pages.purpose: Document all available modes: chat, rlm, godot, james-chat, compile, preflight, doctor, backup, first-run, and their use cases

pages.title: CLI Interface
pages.purpose: Detail command-line argument parsing, configuration options, resume functionality, and passthrough arguments

pages.title: Meeting System
pages.purpose: Explain how the multi-agent meeting system works: orchestration, turn-taking, agent personalities, and response generation

pages.title: Meeting Orchestration
pages.purpose: Document RainLabOrchestrator's lifecycle: initialization, turn-based loop, context management, user intervention, and wrap-up phase

pages.title: Agent Personalities
pages.purpose: Explain the agent system: soul files, agreeableness traits, conversational styles, and how personalities shape responses

pages.title: Response Generation Pipeline
pages.purpose: Detail the LLM interaction pipeline: prompt construction, retry logic, recursive refinement, guardrails, and truncation handling

pages.title: Session Examples
pages.purpose: Showcase real meeting transcripts from RAIN_LAB_MEETING_LOG.md demonstrating multi-agent discussions, topic evolution, and system behavior

pages.title: Communication Channels
pages.purpose: Document the messaging channel integrations that enable external communication via WhatsApp and Telegram

pages.title: Channel Architecture
pages.purpose: Explain the Channel trait, webhook vs. polling models, authentication patterns, and common interfaces

pages.title: WhatsApp Integration
pages.purpose: Document WhatsAppChannel: webhook payload parsing, media download, allowed numbers, and message sending

pages.title: Telegram Integration
pages.purpose: Document TelegramChannel: long-polling, pairing system, streaming updates, draft management, and voice transcription

pages.title: Multimodal Processing
pages.purpose: Explain media handling: image encoding for vision payloads, audio transcription, attachment markers, and content transformations

pages.title: Data Management
pages.purpose: Document persistence systems: logging, checkpointing, metrics collection, and session state management

pages.title: Logging System
pages.purpose: Explain LogManager, VisualEventLogger, and Diplomat: Markdown transcripts, JSONL event streams, log rotation, and file-based IPC

pages.title: Checkpointing & Resume
pages.purpose: Document CheckpointManager: session state snapshots, atomic writes, parse_log_for_resume, and continuation from JSON or Markdown

pages.title: Metrics & Analytics
pages.purpose: Explain MetricsTracker and metrics_log.jsonl: citation accuracy, novel claim density, critique change rate, and session statistics

pages.title: Extended Features
pages.purpose: Document optional capabilities that enhance the meeting experience: voice output, visual avatars, and web search

pages.title: Voice & TTS
pages.purpose: Document VoiceEngine: pyttsx3 integration, character voice mapping, fallback mechanisms, subprocess isolation, and audio export
